title: Eugenia cerasina
intro: Eugenia cerasina, also known as the Myrtle-leaf guava, is a plant species in the family Myrtaceae, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The health benefits of Eugenia cerasina include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, protect against oxidative stress, and combat bacterial infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Eugenia cerasina include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, from digestive issues to skin problems.
taxonomy: Eugenia cerasina is classified within the family Myrtaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the myrtle family. This family includes over 150 genera and more than 3,000 species, many of which are native to Australia and surrounding regions. The genus Eugenia, which includes Eugenia cerasina, is a tropical and subtropical genus found primarily in Central and South America, with some species also found in Africa and Asia. Eugenia cerasina is a species of flowering plant that is native to South America, and its classification reflects its position within the broader Myrtaceae family. The precise classification of Eugenia cerasina at the species level is as follows: Eugenia cerasina is classified within the genus Eugenia, family Myrtaceae, order Myrtales, class Magnoliopsida, and kingdom Plantae.
